title: NOC 94205 – Machine operators and inspectors, electrical apparatus manufacturing | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Canada’s electrical manufacturing sector is a high-growth industry, driven by the expansion of "Smart City" infrastructure and the domestic production of specialized power components. Machine Operators and Inspectors (NOC 94205) are the technical experts who manage the automated equipment used to manufacture high-precision electrical products—from advanced circuit breakers and switchgear to smart meters and specialized lighting systems. As Canada pushes for "Technological Sovereignty," international workers with experience in automated assembly and high-precision inspection are in high demand, particularly in Ontario’s "Silicon Valley North."

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category9 – Occupations in manufacturing and utilitiesTEER Category4 (Usually requiring secondary school and on-the-job training)Major Group94 – Machine operators, assemblers and inspectorsSub-Major Group942 – Assemblers and inspectors in manufacturing

duties: Machine Operation: Setting up and operating automated machinery to manufacture electrical components such as connectors, switches, and relays.
Process Monitoring: Observing machine indicators and digital consoles to ensure production meets specifications and detecting mechanical or electrical malfunctions.
Inspection & Testing: Using digital multimeters, oscilloscopes, and automated test equipment (ATE) to verify the functionality and safety of finished apparatus.
Quality Documentation: Recording production data and logging test results into cloud-based manufacturing execution systems (MES).
2026 Update: Utilizing digital tablets to monitor real-time AI-driven quality sensors and managing "Predictive Maintenance" alerts on connected production lines.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Training: On-the-job training is provided; however, a College Certificate in Electrical Techniques or a similar technical field is highly preferred for 2026 roles.
Manual Dexterity: Strong hand-eye coordination and the ability to work with precision components.
Safety: Mandatory 2026 WHMIS and site-specific high-voltage safety training.
job_outlook: The Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) expects a "Balanced" market. While automation simplifies some repetitive tasks, the complexity of 2026-grade smart apparatus requires more specialized Operators and Inspectors with digital troubleshooting skills. Over 3,000 openings are projected due to the modernization of North America’s electrical grid.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is characterized by "High Skill Demand" as facilities transition to more complex, IoT-enabled electrical apparatus:
Ontario (GTA & Ottawa): Very Good prospects. Ottawa’s telecommunications cluster and the GTA's industrial base are actively recruiting operators for specialized electrical hardware.
Quebec (Montreal): Strong demand. Driven by Hydro-Québec’s infrastructure projects and the expansion of specialized electrical component manufacturing.
British Columbia: Good prospects. Focused on specialized electronics for the green energy and marine technology sectors in the Lower Mainland.
National Trend: Approximately 12,500 people work in this occupation. While basic assembly has largely automated, the demand for Technical Operators to manage these systems remains a top priority.
Why Work in Canada as an Electrical Apparatus Machine Operator?
Lead the Energy Transition: Work on the frontline of the "Smart Grid" revolution, building the components that manage Canada's renewable energy future.
Technological Integration: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including robotic pick-and-place arms, automated wire-stripping systems, and computerized load-testing stations.
Path to PR: Frequently prioritized in Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) streams and eligible for the 2026 TR to PR transition programs as an "Essential Tech-Manufacturing" role.
Stable, Climate-Controlled Work: Most modern electrical apparatus manufacturing is performed in clean, temperature-controlled, and ergonomic environments.
pathways: TR to PR Pathway (April 2026): Electrical apparatus operators are classified as "Essential Manufacturing Workers," making them high-priority candidates for this 33,000-person transition quota.
Ontario Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): This NOC is frequently eligible for provincial nomination for those with a valid job offer in Ontario’s tech and manufacturing corridors.
Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot: A streamlined pathway for technical manufacturing workers to obtain Permanent Residency with a provincial job offer.
OINP Express Entry Human Capital Priorities: Periodically targets candidates with experience in specialized electrical and power manufacturing sectors.
